original_abstract: We introduce NeuroVault, a web-based repository designed to streamline the storage, sharing, visualization, and decoding of statistical maps of the human brain. By leveraging modern web technologies, NeuroVault offers an intuitive interface that provides rich data visualizations directly in the browser, eliminating the need for specialized local software. Furthermore, the platform integrates with the Neurosynth database to enable automated cognitive decoding of uploaded maps. All hosted data are accessible through a public REST API, allowing external tools and services to interface with the repository seamlessly. NeuroVault serves as a comprehensive resource for the neuroscience community, particularly for researchers performing meta-analyses and coactivation studies.
humanized_abstract: This data publication describes NeuroVault, a web site for storing, sharing, visualizing, and analyzing brain mapping data as statistical maps of the brain. NeuroVault employs modern web technologies to give an easy-to-use interface presenting detailed visualizations of the data in the browser without the need for downloading or installing local application software. A large collection of brain maps is also associated with NeuroVault, such as those from group studies found in the Neurosynth database, which are used for the cognitive decoding of uploaded maps. In addition, all of the data on NeuroVault are available via a public REST API, so other web services and applications can use the site as a database. NeuroVault is relevant to any neuroscientist making comparisons of activation, performing meta-analyses, or investigating coactivation patterns.
